Next: Your Hotel Options
Option A · The One You Asked About
Omni Royal Orleans
621 St. Louis Street · French Quarter
This is the Omni you mentioned — a AAA Four Diamond landmark at St. Louis and Royal, one block off Bourbon. Please read my note below before deciding — the property is mid-renovation through spring 2026 and I want you to go in eyes open. Tap the big black price bar to select this hotel.
STARTING FROM · 4 NIGHTS
TAP HERE TO SELECT THIS HOTEL
from$1,095
~$274/night + taxes
Room Type
Deluxe King
Hotel Rating
AAA Four Diamond
Parking
Valet · ~$56/night
Check-in
4:00 PM
Historic 1960
Rib Room Restaurant
Rooftop Pool*
Wrought-Iron Balconies
Pet Friendly
A grand dame at the corner of St. Louis and Royal — historically one of the most prestigious addresses in the Quarter, with classic French Creole detailing and the beloved Rib Room on the ground floor.
⚠ Agent's Note — Please Read Before Selecting
The Omni is in the middle of a multi-phase renovation (the "Chanel-inspired refresh") running through approximately June 30, 2026. Your dates fall inside that window. Recent guest reports from March 2026 note the rooftop pool and fitness center are closed, some common areas are under construction, and noise/dust have been variable. The new Fifi Parée café and reimagined ballroom are open and beautiful, but the full La Riviera rooftop pool bar is not scheduled to reopen until mid-to-late spring 2026 — it may or may not be open during your stay. If the pool and an undisturbed amenities experience matter to you, I'd lean toward Option B or C. If you want the address and don't mind the construction context, Omni is still a lovely choice — but I want you making that call with all the information.
Next: Option B — On Bourbon Street itself
Option B · Right on Bourbon Street
The Royal Sonesta New Orleans
300 Bourbon Street · French Quarter
If "where the action is" means literally on Bourbon Street, this is the one. The Royal Sonesta sits directly on the corner of Bourbon and Bienville — the classic wrought-iron balconies overlook the street, and the Jazz Playhouse inside the hotel is one of the best live music rooms in the Quarter. Tap the big black price bar to select this hotel.
STARTING FROM · 4 NIGHTS
TAP HERE TO SELECT THIS HOTEL
from$1,275
~$319/night + taxes
Room Type
Deluxe King
Hotel Rating
AAA Four Diamond
Parking
Valet · ~$55/night
Check-in
4:00 PM
Directly on Bourbon
Outdoor Pool Open
Jazz Playhouse On-Site
Restaurant R'evolution
Desire Oyster Bar
Bourbon Street's most refined address — 483 rooms wrapped around a lush courtyard and heated pool, with four on-site restaurants and bars so you can step outside when you want to and retreat into a quiet garden when you don't.
Agent's Note
For "where the action is," this is my top pick for you. The hotel is fully operational (no renovation noise), and because you arrive May 7 — four days after Jazz Fest ends — rates ease significantly versus the late-April peak but the city is still buzzing. I'd request a courtyard-facing room rather than a Bourbon-facing balcony room — you'll still feel the energy from your balcony over coffee, but you'll actually sleep at night. The Jazz Playhouse inside the hotel has a free nightly show worth planning around.
Next: Option C — The Carousel Bar hotel
Option C · The Iconic Carousel Bar
Hotel Monteleone
214 Royal Street · French Quarter
Family-owned since 1886, the Monteleone is the French Quarter's grand old soul — one block off Bourbon on the tamer Royal Street, home of the Carousel Bar (yes, it actually rotates) and a rooftop pool that's open. The "quietest Bourbon-adjacent hotel" pick. Tap the big black price bar to select this hotel.
STARTING FROM · 4 NIGHTS
TAP HERE TO SELECT THIS HOTEL
from$1,155
~$289/night + taxes
Room Type
Deluxe King
Hotel Rating
4 Star · Historic
Parking
Valet · ~$54/night
Check-in
4:00 PM
Est. 1886 · Family Owned
Carousel Bar
Heated Rooftop Pool
Spa Aria
Criollo Restaurant
A Beaux-Arts landmark where writers from Faulkner to Capote have stayed — ornate, proudly vintage, and just one block from Bourbon but markedly quieter, with a rooftop pool overlooking the river.
Agent's Note
If you want full French Quarter character without sleeping on top of the nightlife, Monteleone is the classic answer — and the Carousel Bar alone is worth the price of admission. Rooms run on the compact side (a few guests have noted this), so I'd request a premier or deluxe room on a higher floor with a street-facing window to avoid the smaller interior rooms facing the airshaft. Zero renovation disruption here — everything is operational.
Next: Dining & things to do
Explore More

Dining & Experiences

A short list of my favorites, all within a 10-minute walk of any of the three hotels. Tap any that interest you and I'll handle reservations and timing before you go. Every option shows a price range so you know what you're signing up for.
DINING — CLASSIC FRENCH QUARTER
Antoine's
Oldest restaurant in NOLA (est. 1840) · Oysters Rockefeller was invented here · ~$50–90/pp
Commander's Palace
Garden District · Jazz Brunch on Saturday is legendary · 25¢ martinis at lunch · ~$60–100/pp
GW Fins
Best seafood in the Quarter · Menu changes daily with the catch · ~$55–85/pp
Café du Monde
Beignets & café au lait at the French Market · Cash only · Open 24/7 · ~$8–12/pp
Galatoire's
Bourbon Street institution · Friday lunch is the scene · ~$55–95/pp
LIVE MUSIC & NIGHTLIFE
Preservation Hall
The temple of traditional New Orleans jazz · 3–4 shows nightly · ~$25–50/pp
The Spotted Cat
Frenchmen Street · Local musicians, intimate room, cash bar · No cover, 1-drink min · ~$15–25/pp
Pat O'Brien's
Home of the original Hurricane cocktail · Dueling pianos · ~$15–30/pp
ATTRACTIONS & EXPERIENCES
Steamboat Natchez Jazz Cruise
2-hour river cruise with live jazz & optional Creole dinner · ~$49 cruise / ~$85 w/ dinner
French Quarter History Walking Tour
2 hours with a licensed guide · Architecture, voodoo, pirates, ghosts · ~$30–40/pp
New Orleans Museum of Art & Sculpture Garden
City Park · Beautiful escape from the Quarter · ~$15 admission · Free Wed for LA residents
St. Charles Streetcar to Garden District
$1.25 each way · Historic mansions, Lafayette Cemetery, Magazine Street shopping
